item.title: NSW stun QLD 22-20 to make history in ‘greatest escape’ Origin has ever seen
All Blacks captain Scott Barrett ruled out of rugby for three to five months
FIFA ordered to explain World Cup ticket pricing
Who will wear the No.1 jersey for the Maroons in Origin Game 1
Injury blow for Blues as Moses suffers a suspected hamstring injury
Bula Boys coach names provisional 21-member squad for international friendlies
item.description: Fiji national men’s football team head coach Stephane Auvray has named a provisional 21-member squad for the Fiji men’s national football team’s two international friendly matches against Vanuatu national football team during the June FIFA Window in Port Vila. Auvray says the friendlies will serve as an important opportunity to assess players he has not yet worked with since taking charge of the n
item.content:encoded:
Fiji national men’s football team head coach Stephane Auvray has named a provisional 21-member squad for the Fiji men’s national football team’s two international friendly matches against Vanuatu national football team during the June FIFA Window in Port Vila.

Auvray says the friendlies will serve as an important opportunity to assess players he has not yet worked with since taking charge of the national side.

He says the national team is still in a building phase and the focus remains on long-term development rather than immediate results.

Auvray says the coaching staff will use the matches to identify the best combinations, formations and playing structures ahead of future major competitions, with 2029 being the long-term target.

He also highlights the importance of integrating Bula FC players into the national setup, saying their understanding of his coaching methods and professionalism will help strengthen the team culture.

Auvray says the short preparation period means the camp will focus more on building a strong foundation while giving players freedom to express themselves on the field.

The coach also reveals he hopes several young players impress during camp, including 17-year-old Maikah Dau , who he says continues to show promising potential.

The team will enter camp in Ba this Sunday before departing for Vanuatu for the two-match series.

The provisional squad includes Melvin Prakash, Ilisoni Logaivau, Asivorosi Rabo, Scott Wara, Gabiriele Matainisiga, Semi Nabenu, Lekima Gonerau, Praneel Naidu, Ivan Kumar, Tevita Warainivalu, Ryan Naresh, Maikah Dau, Josaia Sela, Setaraki Hughes, Solayman Mohammed, Roy Krishna , Rusiate Doidoi, Zachariah Harang, Ibraheem Afazal, Etonia Dogalau and Isikeli Sevanaia.

item.title: Rabitu moves to centre while Armstrong-Ravula drops to fullback
State of Origin Game 1 tonight at 10.05pm
item.description: Rugby league’s fiercest rivalry reignites tonight as New South Wales Blues and Queensland Maroons clash in Game I of the 2026 men’s State of Origin series in Sydney. With pride, passion and state supremacy on the line, the opening battle at Stadium Australia is expected to deliver another brutal chapter in one of sport’s greatest rivalries.
item.content:encoded:
Rugby league’s fiercest rivalry reignites tonight as New South Wales Blues and Queensland Maroons clash in Game I of the 2026 men’s State of Origin series in Sydney.

With pride, passion and state supremacy on the line, the opening battle at Stadium Australia is expected to deliver another brutal chapter in one of sport’s greatest rivalries.

Queensland enter the series as defending champions after their memorable 2025 triumph, where the Maroons stunned the Blues with a 24-12 victory in the decider on New South Wales soil to reclaim the shield.

The Maroons have also dominated recent encounters in enemy territory, including a crushing 38-10 victory in Game I of the 2024 series at the same venue.

Now, New South Wales will be desperate to respond in front of a packed home crowd and begin their campaign with a statement victory, while Queensland will look to continue their remarkable record away from home.

Meanwhile, New South Wales Blues have been dealt a major blow ahead of Game I, with Mitchell Moses ruled out on the eve of the clash due to a hamstring injury.

Ethan Strange has been promoted from the bench to partner Nathan Cleary in the halves, while Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs utility Matt Burton has been called into the squad and added to the interchange bench.

Game I kicks off at 10.05pm FJT in Sydney tonight.

item.title: Fiji Men’s team heartbreak still fresh as HSBC 7s returns to Spain
item.description: As the HSBC SVNS World Championship returns to Spain, memories remain fresh from the last tournament, where the Fiji Airways Men’s National 7s team fell to France 28–7 in the dramatic 2024 Olympic gold medal final. The last time the HSBC SVNS was held in Spain in 2024, the tournament in Madrid delivered some of the most memorable moments in sevens rugby and left fans talking long after the final w
item.content:encoded:
As the HSBC SVNS World Championship returns to Spain, memories remain fresh from the last tournament, where the Fiji Airways Men’s National 7s team fell to France 28–7 in the dramatic 2024 Olympic gold medal final.

The last time the HSBC SVNS was held in Spain in 2024, the tournament in Madrid delivered some of the most memorable moments in sevens rugby and left fans talking long after the final whistle.

Home fans had plenty to celebrate as both Spain’s men’s and women’s teams successfully avoided relegation and secured their places as core teams for the following season.

Kenya supporters also created one of the biggest atmospheres of the tournament, with the famous “Kenya Corner” becoming one of the standout attractions as Shujaa secured promotion with passionate support from fans throughout the weekend.

French star Antoine Dupont also made a huge impact in sevens rugby, helping lift France to another level and becoming one of the biggest talking points of the competition.

Now, with the HSBC SVNS World Championship returning to Spain this week in Valladolid, organisers and fans will be hoping for another memorable tournament.

Fiji are pooled with Spain, France and the USA.

Meanwhile, Fiji will begin its Valladolid 7s campaign against France on Friday at 11.40pm before facing the USA at 4.24am on Saturday.

They will then take on hosts Spain at 12.12am on Sunday in their final pool match.

item.title: Men in Black aiming to reclaim former glory at Fiji FACT
New York Knicks storm into NBA Finals for first time since 1999 after sweeping Cleveland Cavaliers 4-0
item.description: Karl-Anthony Towns scored 19 points and grabbed 14 rebounds, while OG Anunoby added 17 points as the New York Knicks crushed the Cleveland Cavaliers 130-93 to complete a 4-0 series sweep and reach the NBA Finals for the first time since 1999. Landry Shamet scored 16 off the bench while Mikal Bridges and Jalen Brunson had 15 apiece for the Knicks, who became the fourth team to have an 11-game winni
item.content:encoded:
Karl-Anthony Towns scored 19 points and grabbed 14 rebounds, while OG Anunoby added 17 points as the New York Knicks crushed the Cleveland Cavaliers 130-93 to complete a 4-0 series sweep and reach the NBA Finals for the first time since 1999.

Landry Shamet scored 16 off the bench while Mikal Bridges and Jalen Brunson had 15 apiece for the Knicks, who became the fourth team to have an 11-game winning streak during their postseason run.

The last to do it was Golden State, which had a 15-game run en route to its second title in three seasons in 2017.

The Knicks pulled their starters with 7:47 remaining and a 35-point lead as their large contingent of fans loudly chanted “Knicks in four!” New York fans easily outnumbered Cleveland fans as die-hard celebrity fans director Spike Lee, comedian Tracy Morgan and actor Timothée Chalamet and his girlfriend, Kylie Jenner, made the trip.

New York will play the defending champion Oklahoma City Thunder or San Antonio Spurs in the Finals.

The Western Conference finals is tied at two games apiece with Game 5 to be played in Oklahoma City on tomorrow.

The Thunder or Spurs will have homecourt advantage when the Finals start on June 4th because of a better regular-season record.

This will be the Knicks’ third appearance in the Finals since winning their last title in 1973.

They lost in seven games to Houston in 1994 and in five to San Antonio in 1999. Source: CNN

item.title: Valladolid 7s: Kolinisau says Fiji must take its chances at World Championship
item.description: Ahead of the World Championship in Valladolid Spain this weekend, Fiji Airways National Men's 7s coach Osea Kolinisau is stressing to his team that 7s is a game of opportunities. Kolinisau highlighted this to fijivillage Sports saying they can be the fittest, the favourites, or the most skilful team but this game is about taking opportunities and they have to take their chances.
item.content:encoded:
Ahead of the World Championship in Valladolid Spain this weekend, Fiji Airways National Men's 7s coach Osea Kolinisau is stressing to his team that 7s is a game of opportunities.

Kolinisau highlighted this to fijivillage Sports saying they can be the fittest, the favourites, or the most skilful team but this game is about taking opportunities and they have to take their chances.

Insert: Kolinisau on opportunities May 26

The Men's team kick off their campaign on Friday against France at 11.40pm.

They then play the USA at 4.24am Saturday before facing hosts Spain at 12.12am Sunday.

The Men's Cup quarter finals kick off at 2.27am Sunday, the semi finals are at 8.44pm Sunday with the final at 2.26am next Monday.

The Fijiana 7s will play the USA at 8pm Friday, they then meet Australia at 12.51am Saturday before facing South Africa at 8pm.

item.title: More than 700 students to take part in National School Swimming Championships this weekend
item.description: More than 700 students will be jumping into the pool for this weekend's National Schools Swimming Championships. National Primary and Secondary Schools Swimming Association President, Salaseini Lele says swimmers from over 54 schools are taking part this year.
item.content:encoded:
More than 700 students will be jumping into the pool for this weekend's National Schools Swimming Championships.

National Primary and Secondary Schools Swimming Association President, Salaseini Lele says swimmers from over 54 schools are taking part this year.

Lele says 9 swimmers that took part in the recent Oceania Swimming Championship will be representing their schools this weekend.

She says swimmers like Jai Narayan College's Grace Khelan, Swami Vivekananda College's Reuben Taylor and Jeremiah Rova, as well as Thaddeus Kwong who is home schooled, will be taking part.

Marist Brothers High School are the defending champions in the boys' division while Drasa Avenue School currently hold the girls' title.

Competition starts at 8.30am Friday at the National Aquatic Centre.

item.title: Highlanders prop Daniel Lienert-Brown handed four-week suspension
item.description: Highlanders prop Daniel Lienert-Brown has received a four-week suspension after being found guilty of a dangerous tackle in contravention of Law 9.13. The dangerous play, for which Lienert-Brown received a red card, occurred in the 7th minute of last Saturday's match between the Hurricanes and Highlanders at Hnry Stadium in Wellington.
item.content:encoded:
Highlanders prop Daniel Lienert-Brown has received a four-week suspension after being found guilty of a dangerous tackle in contravention of Law 9.13.

The dangerous play, for which Lienert-Brown received a red card, occurred in the 7th minute of last Saturday's match between the Hurricanes and Highlanders at Hnry Stadium in Wellington.

The Foul Play Review Committee (FPRC) determined that the act of foul play merited a mid-range entry point of six weeks/matches.

In light of Lienert-Brown's acceptance that he committed an act of foul play, as well as mitigating factors such as his recent disciplinary record and remorse, the FPRC applied a reduction in the sanction of two weeks/matches.

Lienert-Brown has been suspended from all forms of the game for four weeks/matches.

The player was granted approval to apply to the World Rugby coaching intervention; however, he has already completed the course in 2026 for a ruck infringement, and it will be at the discretion of World Rugby to accept his application.
